facts_disclosed: Cancelled Sky Sports — switched to DAZN, much cheaper

turns.content: so I did something I never thought id do. cancelled Sky Sports
That's a big move! What made you finally decide to pull the plug on it?

honestly the price mate. its been going up every year and Simran has been on at me about it for ages saying we dont need it. and shes right innit. we were paying like 50 quid a month just for the sports package on top of everything else

Fifty quid a month just for the sports add-on is steep, especially when it keeps creeping up. That's six hundred a year, which is real money. Are you going without sports coverage entirely or did you find an alternative?
nah course not haha. cant miss the football. switched to DAZN. its like half the price and it covers the Premier League and Champions League which is basically all I watch anyway

Smart move. DAZN has really expanded their Premier League coverage and for someone who's mainly watching football, it covers the essentials at a much better price point. The interface is decent too and you can watch on your phone between jobs if you need to catch a match.

Are you getting the annual plan or paying monthly?

went annual cos its cheaper per month that way. locked it in. Simran was happy cos shes been saying for about two years we should cancel sky and I kept saying no no I need it. turns out I didnt need it I just needed the football

Ha, she was right all along and now you've proved it yourself -- that's the worst kind of being wrong because you can't even argue. But honestly the annual lock-in is the smart play, you get the best rate and you know exactly what you're paying. No surprise price hikes mid-year.

Does it work okay on your TV or do you need to cast it from a phone?

works on the smart TV no problem. downloaded the app and it was sorted in like 5 minutes. honestly the picture quality is just as good I cant tell the difference. and Arjun didnt even notice we switched. he just wants to watch Man City whoever is showing it

If Arjun can't tell the difference then that's the ultimate test passed right there -- kids are usually the first to complain when something changes. And if the picture quality holds up on the TV, you're genuinely getting the same experience for half the money. That's just smart budgeting.

What are you planning to do with the savings? Or is it just going into the general pot?

general pot for now. weve been trying to be a bit smarter with money lately. the new van was a big spend even with the deal so were tightening up on the stuff that doesnt matter. sky was the obvious one

That's a sensible approach -- after a big purchase, trimming the subscriptions and recurring costs is the easiest way to claw back some breathing room without actually changing your lifestyle. And you've proven with this one that cutting doesn't have to mean going without. You're watching the same matches, the family hasn't noticed, and you're keeping an extra few hundred quid a year.

Are there any other subscriptions on the hit list or was Sky the main one?

sky was the big one. weve got Netflix but thats like 11 quid which is fine and Simran uses it loads. other than that its just the usual stuff. I'm not one of those people with 15 different streaming things. two is enough innit

Two is perfectly reasonable -- one for sports, one for everything else. You've got it covered without the overlap and waste. Some people are paying for four or five services and watching one show on each. Keeping it to DAZN and Netflix is clean and simple.

Sounds like Simran will be pleased you finally listened. Better late than never, right?
haha yeah shes already done the I told you so. I said fair enough you were right. happy wife happy life and all that. brilliant though honestly dont know why I didnt do it sooner

The important thing is you did it and the result speaks for itself -- same football, half the price, and domestic harmony restored. Can't ask for more than that. Enjoy the Man City matches on DAZN this season!
